theme: break the disk into exactly k petals
title: Petal Press: Dispersion-Tuned Disk Fission
strategies: identity/no-op kernel with zero bias: no spatial coupling at all, so the ring's final sign pattern tracks raw noise crossings -- essentially never lands on any specific target k (reproduces the evaluator's weak 'naive' reference)

generic local-averaging/diffusive kernel (positive weights decaying with offset) plus one strong localized bias bump spent on the full budget: solves the k=1 case outright (a diffusive kernel's dispersion relation always favors the lowest surviving wavenumber under the zero-mean dynamics) but cannot be steered to any other target k, however the single bump is aimed

dispersion-shaped kernel: set kernel taps to a cosine tuned to the target wavenumber, w_j = A*cos(2*pi*k*j/N), so the linear growth-rate curve (the kernel's own Fourier transform) peaks at m=k and that mode robustly outgrows all competitors across the hidden noise ensemble before the cubic term saturates it -- no bias injection needed

windowed / tap-budgeted refinements: taper the cosine kernel (e.g. a raised-cosine envelope) or trade off amplitude against the allowed number of taps to avoid overshoot instabilities while keeping the growth-rate peak sharp, squeezing out additional robustness within the same L_max, W_max, B budget
